execution_order.action: Pull Batch 5 send list from your 2026-05-12 session records
execution_order.note: Every lead emailed 2026-05-12 is a LinkedIn candidate. Full name, business name, town, signal, sector required. Without this list: sector_discovery_fallback_queries below are the fallback — use town+sector discovery approach.

execution_order.action: IMAP sweep — ALL Batch 5 leads (NOT leads 1/3/22). Per lead, not bulk.
execution_order.note: FIRST THING Monday morning — before any LinkedIn search. Per lead. If NOT_INTERESTED: block LinkedIn permanently, cancel Day-7 send. If UNSUBSCRIBE: suppress all channels within 24h (GDPR Article 21). If INTERESTED: LinkedIn still valid — proceed with note, do NOT reference email in note. If OOO: LinkedIn still valid — proceed.

execution_order.action: Execute LinkedIn searches in sector priority order: Solicitors → Estate agents → Accountants → Hotels/B&Bs → Restaurants → Boutique retail → Trades
execution_order.note: Primary query format: site:linkedin.com/in "[Full Name]" "[Business Name]". If no result: use fallback chain. If surname unknown: use sector_discovery_fallback_queries for that town+sector.

execution_order.action: Check posts from last 60 days on EVERY confirmed profile BEFORE drafting note
execution_order.note: Post-reference note has 3-4x higher acceptance. Do not skip. Templates in linkedin_connection_notes.json (monday_fallback_notes section).

execution_order.action: Send connection request with note — use monday_fallback_notes from linkedin_connection_notes.json
execution_order.note: 200-char HARD limit. Count in plain-text editor before sending. Do NOT mention email, SSL, mobile, 'Not Secure', website audit, or any prior contact.

execution_order.action: Write data/linkedin_profiles.json IMMEDIATELY after each search
execution_order.note: MANDATORY. One entry per lead searched. Do not batch — write each entry immediately after the search, whether or not a profile was found. This is the ONLY confirmation mechanism. Without this file: archive trigger fires Tuesday EOD for EVERY Batch 5 lead.

execution_order.action: CHANGELOG.md update — MANDATORY before Tuesday Day-7 sends
execution_order.note: 51 days overdue. Log: all Batch 5 Day-0 sends, Day-3 bumps, LinkedIn outcomes (confirmed or PROFILE_NOT_FOUND), all IMAP results since April, archive decisions. Must complete before Tuesday 2026-05-19 sends.

rules: No LinkedIn on weekends — Monday is valid
No LinkedIn to leads 1/3/22/2/7/19/49/51/53/54 — permanently blocked
IMAP per lead before note — mandatory
200-character hard limit on every note — count in plain text editor
Do NOT reference the email sequence, SSL, mobile, audit findings, or 'Not Secure' in any note
Do NOT start a note with 'I'
If owner surname is NOT confirmed: skip LinkedIn for that lead (no guessing profiles)
Write profiles.json entry after EVERY search — not just confirmed profiles

search_queries_status: PENDING_HANDS_POPULATION — HANDS must populate this array from their 2026-05-12 Batch 5 send records. Format: site:linkedin.com/in "[Full Name]" "[Business Name]". If name unknown: use sector_discovery_fallback_queries below. Execute Monday 2026-05-18.

search_queries_format.description: Once HANDS confirms the Batch 5 send list, populate search_queries[] with entries in this format:

primary_query_format.instruction: For each Batch 5 lead where you have a full name (first + surname confirmed), use this as your primary query before any fallback:
fallback_chain: Fallback 2: Use sector_discovery_fallback_queries below for that town+sector
Fallback 3: Skip — do NOT send note to a profile you cannot confidently identify as the owner. Log as PROFILE_NOT_FOUND in profiles.json.

sector_discovery_fallback_queries.note: Use these when: (1) name-based primary query yields no result, OR (2) owner surname is unknown. Execute in the order shown — sector priority applies within each town. Today: Day-3 bumps assumed sent Friday. Monday is FINAL window. Archives fire Tuesday EOD.

Malton.query: site:linkedin.com/in solicitor "Malton" "North Yorkshire" (director OR partner OR owner)
Malton.note: Highest LinkedIn likelihood in Ryedale. Malton solicitors almost always have profiles. Check posts from last 60 days — legal updates, charity, local events. Post-reference is very likely.

Malton.query: site:linkedin.com/in "estate agent" "Malton" "North Yorkshire" (director OR owner OR manager)
Malton.note: Estate agents in Malton post market updates and sold listings. High post-reference opportunity.

Malton.query: site:linkedin.com/in accountant "Malton" "North Yorkshire" (director OR partner OR owner)
Malton.note: High LinkedIn presence. Post frequently about tax deadlines, Making Tax Digital.

Malton.query: site:linkedin.com/in "Malton" "North Yorkshire" retail (owner OR director OR founder)
Malton.note: Malton has a strong independent food and retail scene. Food Festival presence often generates posts.

Malton.query: site:linkedin.com/in "[Full Name]" "Malton" (plumber OR electrician OR builder OR joiner OR roofer)
Malton.note: Trades — LOW LinkedIn presence. Only search if you have a full name confirmed. Skip if surname unknown.

Helmsley.query: site:linkedin.com/in hotel "Helmsley" "North Yorkshire" (owner OR director OR manager)
Helmsley.note: Highest post-reference likelihood in the entire Ryedale batch. Helmsley hospitality owners post about seasonal events, local walks, NYM Park. Check posts FIRST — always.

Helmsley.query: site:linkedin.com/in restaurant "Helmsley" (owner OR chef OR director)
Helmsley.note: Fine dining in the Helmsley/Harome area. Owners post menus, seasonal ingredients, supplier stories. Post-reference very likely.
Helmsley estate agents cover the wider Ryedale rural market — active LinkedIn presences.

Helmsley.query: site:linkedin.com/in "Helmsley" retail (owner OR director OR founder)
Helmsley.note: Independent shops — medium presence. Helmsley market day generates some activity.

Pickering.query: site:linkedin.com/in (hotel OR "B&B" OR "bed and breakfast") "Pickering" "North Yorkshire" (owner OR director)
Pickering.note: Pickering is a gateway to the North York Moors and NYMR. B&B and hotel owners post about tourism, the steam railway, seasonal offers.

Pickering.query: site:linkedin.com/in "estate agent" "Pickering" (director OR owner OR manager)
Pickering.note: Medium presence. Rural property market generates some posting activity.
Modest LinkedIn presence for Pickering restaurants — generic note if profile found.

Pickering.query: site:linkedin.com/in "[Full Name]" "Pickering" (builder OR plumber OR joiner OR electrician)
Pickering.note: Trades — LOW. Only if full name confirmed. Skip if surname unknown.

Kirkbymoorside.query: site:linkedin.com/in "Kirkbymoorside" "North Yorkshire" (solicitor OR accountant OR estate agent) (owner OR director OR partner)
Kirkbymoorside.note: Try BOTH spellings — 'Kirkbymoorside' and 'Kirbymoorside' both appear in LinkedIn profiles. Small town — cast a slightly wider net.

Kirkbymoorside.query: site:linkedin.com/in "Kirkbymoorside" (hotel OR retail OR restaurant) owner director
Kirkbymoorside.note: Small market town. LinkedIn presence is modest. Use generic note if profile found.

Hovingham.query: site:linkedin.com/in "Hovingham" "North Yorkshire" (owner OR director OR manager)
Hovingham.note: Very small village. LinkedIn presence is LOW. The Worsley Arms Hotel is the primary hospitality lead — search specifically if in your send list. Generic note only — post-reference unlikely.

Norton-on-Derwent.query: site:linkedin.com/in "Norton" "Malton" (solicitor OR accountant OR estate agent OR builder) (owner OR director OR partner)
Norton-on-Derwent.note: Norton is effectively part of the Malton market area. Use 'Norton' 'Malton' in queries — the full hyphenated 'Norton-on-Derwent' rarely appears in LinkedIn profiles.

Norton-on-Derwent.query: site:linkedin.com/in "[Full Name]" "Norton" "Malton" (builder OR plumber OR joiner OR roofer)
Norton-on-Derwent.note: Trades — LOW LinkedIn presence. Full name required. Skip if surname unknown.
